project.title: NIDAN-AI
project.subtitle: Portable Multi-Modal AI-Based Fever Triage and Explainable Health Risk Assessment Platform
project.target_feel: Real healthcare startup product — not a college project, not a chatbot, not a flashy AI demo

core_objective.description: Build a clean, professional, mobile-first AI-powered healthcare triage platform
primary_functions: Google Sign-In patient authentication with persistent history via Supabase
Sensor-based physiological monitoring from ESP32 hardware with manual entry fallback
Machine learning risk level and infection pattern prediction
Gemini API as the primary explainability and reasoning layer (hidden, internal only)
Daily and longitudinal fever trend tracking with interactive charts
Emergency alert system with threshold-based triggers
Smart health recommendations based on triage output
Nearby doctor/clinic discovery filtered by detected fever type
core_objective.is_a: First-level health triage assistant for pre-clinical symptom assessment

frontend.maps: Google Maps JavaScript API (for nearby doctors)

backend.hardware_proxy: FastAPI endpoints acting as relay to ESP32 HTTP server

database.realtime: Supabase Realtime for live sensor data updates
database.storage: Supabase Storage (for PPG signal data if needed)

hardware.data_flow: Sensors → ESP32 → WiFi → FastAPI → Supabase → React Dashboard

overall_aesthetic.direction: Refined medical minimalism — like the reference app images provided
overall_aesthetic.feel: Premium healthcare app (think: Apple Health meets clinical dashboard)
overall_aesthetic.inspiration: The reference image shows white cards, navy/dark-blue CTAs, soft shadows, real doctor photos, clean date pickers, pill-shaped time selectors, bottom navigation bar

strictly_avoid: Dark mode as primary (light mode first)

color_palette.primary_accent: #1B4FD8 (medical navy blue — matches reference image CTAs)

typography.primary_font: DM Sans (Google Fonts) — clean, modern, medical

animations.card_entry: staggered fade-in from bottom (delay: 0.1s per card)
animations.health_meter: animated progress bar fill on mount
animations.pulse_indicator: CSS keyframe ping animation on critical alerts

mobile_first.bottom_navigation: Fixed bottom nav bar on mobile (Home, Scan, Reports, Profile)
mobile_first.top_bar: Sticky header with greeting and notification bell (matches reference)

flow: On success, Supabase session is established
patient_auth.profile_creation: On first Google login, insert row into public.patients table with google sub, name, email, avatar_url

role_detection.routing: On login, check role → redirect to /dashboard or /doctor/dashboard

medical_history.patient_id: uuid REFERENCES patients(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

scans.patient_id: uuid REFERENCES patients(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

daily_trends.patient_id: uuid REFERENCES patients(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

nearby_doctor_results.scan_id: uuid REFERENCES scans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
nearby_doctor_results.patient_id: uuid REFERENCES patients(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

row_level_security.patients: Patient can only read/write own row
row_level_security.medical_history: Patient can only read/write own medical history
row_level_security.scans: Patient can read own scans; doctors can read scans of their patients
row_level_security.daily_trends: Patient can only read own trends
row_level_security.doctors: Doctors can read/write own row; patients can read verified doctors

supabase_realtime.use_case: Live dashboard updates when ESP32 pushes new sensor data

complete_scan_flow.description: Patient opens app, sees clean medical landing page with NIDAN-AI branding
Personalized greeting with first name, last scan summary, quick action card to start new scan
Full-page form for medical background — saved to Supabase medical_history table
fields: Existing medical conditions (large textarea with placeholder examples)
complete_scan_flow.behavior: If patient already has saved medical history, pre-populate fields. Allow update.

complete_scan_flow.description: Multi-section symptom collection — clean pill-chip selector UI per symptom
Fetch live sensor data from ESP32 OR manually enter values

fetch_from_hardware.description: Button triggers GET request to ESP32 endpoint
fetch_from_hardware.ui: Loading spinner → animated card reveal with live values
fetch_from_hardware.error_handling: If fetch fails, auto-switch to manual entry with toast notification

manual_entry.description: User manually inputs all sensor values
manual_entry.validation: Real-time range validation with color-coded feedback (green=normal, amber=borderline, red=abnormal)

complete_scan_flow.toggle_ui: Tab switcher at top: [📡 Fetch from Device] [✏️ Enter Manually]

complete_scan_flow.description: Optional but encouraged step — camera-based pulse and HRV proxy analysis
ui_flow: Instruction card: 'Place your fingertip firmly over the rear camera lens'
Live intensity graph updating in real-time (Recharts LineChart)
On complete: HRV proxy calculated and shown
Skip option available (clearly labeled as optional)

ppg_processing.method: Capture video frames → extract green channel pixel average → analyze peak intervals → compute HRV proxy (RMSSD approximation)
output_fields: ppg_raw_signal (array of intensity values over time)
ppg_processing.storage: Stored in scans table as jsonb

complete_scan_flow.description: Backend processing screen — animated loading states
backend_actions: Gemini API → Generate explainable reasoning + recommendations (internal only)
Save scan to Supabase scans table
Return structured result JSON to frontend

complete_scan_flow.description: Complete triage output — the most important page

login_screen.layout: Full-screen centered layout, light blue-tinted background
elements: NIDAN-AI logo + tagline: 'Smart Health Triage, Powered by AI'
Large 'Continue with Google' button (white pill with Google icon) — primary CTA
Email + Password fields (for patients who registered manually)
Link: 'Are you a doctor? Doctor Login'
login_screen.design_notes: No heavy background image — soft gradient mesh in teal/blue tones. Google button must look exactly like official Google sign-in button style.

register_screen.note: Only for manual registration fallback — Google users auto-register

sections.name: Top Header
content: Right: Notification bell icon with badge count
Below greeting: 'Here is your health summary'

sections.name: Active Alert Banner
sections.condition: If last scan has critical/high alerts
sections.content: Red/amber dismissible banner with alert message and 'View Details' button

sections.name: Last Scan Summary Card
content: Temperature, SpO2, HR in small chips

sections.name: Quick Stats Row
sections.layout: 2x2 grid of metric cards (like reference image)

sections.name: Start New Scan CTA
sections.content: Large full-width navy blue button: '+ Start New Health Scan'
sections.style: Navy blue (#1B4FD8) with white text, 14px border-radius, subtle shadow

sections.name: Recent Scans List
sections.content: Last 3 scans as clickable cards showing date, risk level, and fever type

medical_history_screen.layout: Scrollable form with sticky progress indicator
medical_history_screen.progress: Step 1 of 4 (pill-style progress bar at top)
medical_history_screen.subheader: This helps us understand your health background

form_sections.label: Do you have any existing medical conditions?
form_sections.placeholder: e.g. Type 2 Diabetes, Asthma, Hypertension, Thyroid disorder, Previous dengue or malaria...

form_sections.label: Any known allergies?
form_sections.placeholder: e.g. Penicillin allergy, Sulfa drugs, Dust allergy, Food allergies...

form_sections.label: Are you currently taking any medications?
form_sections.placeholder: e.g. Metformin 500mg twice daily, Salbutamol inhaler, Paracetamol as needed...

form_sections.label: Any past surgeries or hospitalizations?
form_sections.placeholder: e.g. Appendectomy 2019, Hospitalized for dengue fever 2022...

form_sections.label: Any relevant family medical history?
form_sections.placeholder: e.g. Father has Type 2 Diabetes, Mother has hypertension...

form_sections.label: Anything else you'd like us to know?
form_sections.placeholder: e.g. I'm pregnant, I recently donated blood, I'm immunocompromised...

medical_history_screen.note: Medical history is stored separately and is NOT passed to ML models. It is used by Gemini for context-aware reasoning and by the doctor dashboard.

fields.label: How long have you had fever?

section_2_symptoms.title: Current Symptoms
section_2_symptoms.subtitle: Select the severity of each symptom you're experiencing
section_2_symptoms.ui_pattern: One card per symptom with 4 pill chips horizontally

symptoms.question: How severe is your headache?
symptoms.description: Include any throbbing, pressure, or location-specific pain

symptoms.question: How severe is your cough?
symptoms.description: Include dry cough, wet cough, or persistent coughing fits

symptoms.question: Have you experienced vomiting or nausea?
Are you experiencing body or muscle pain?
symptoms.description: Include joint pain, body aches, or generalized soreness

symptoms.question: Have you noticed skin rash or red spots?
symptoms.description: Include any unusual skin changes, spots, or irritation

symptoms.question: Have you experienced chills or shivering?
symptoms.description: Include sudden cold feelings despite high temperature

symptoms.question: Are you experiencing unusual sweating?
symptoms.description: Include night sweats or excessive perspiration

symptoms.question: Have you recently traveled to unfamiliar or high-risk areas?
symptoms.description: Travel in the past 14 days that may have involved exposure

questionnaire_screen.ml_mapping_note: Only binary values (0/1) are sent to ML models. Severity scores (0-3) are stored in Supabase for analytics and trend visualization.

hardware_screen.subheader: Collect your vital signs from the device or enter manually

tab_1.label: 📡 Fetch from Device
tab_1.description: Automatically pulls data from your connected ESP32 sensor device

tab_2.label: ✏️ Manual Entry
tab_2.description: Enter your vital signs manually if device is unavailable

status_states.idle: Device icon with 'Connect & Fetch Data' button
status_states.success: Green check: animated card reveal of all four values
status_states.error: Red icon: 'Could not connect. Please enter manually.' — auto-toggle to manual tab

ppg_screen.subheader: Optional — Place your fingertip on the rear camera
ppg_screen.optional_badge: Shown as 'OPTIONAL' pill badge in amber

content: Animated illustration of finger on camera
Instruction: 'Place your fingertip firmly over the rear camera lens. Keep still for 60 seconds.'
Secondary tip: 'Make sure your environment is well-lit or enable flash'

ui_flow.content: Camera permission dialog — custom styled explanation card before browser prompt

content: Circular countdown timer (60 seconds) with animated ring in teal
Live intensity waveform below (Recharts LineChart, last 100 samples)
Text: 'Detecting pulse... Keep your finger steady'

ui_flow.content: Error card with retry or skip option

technical_implementation.flash: ImageCapture API or track.applyConstraints({ advanced: [{ torch: true }] })
technical_implementation.frame_capture: requestAnimationFrame → drawImage to canvas → getImageData → extract green channel mean
technical_implementation.peak_detection: Simple sliding window peak detection on green channel signal
technical_implementation.storage: Save raw signal array (sampled at ~30fps, 60s = ~1800 points) as jsonb in scans.ppg_raw_signal

results_screen.subheader: Based on your symptoms and vitals

sections.name: Triage Level Card
sections.description: The most prominent element on the page — full-width colored banner

High.message: Please consult a doctor within 24 hours.

sections.name: Infection Pattern Analysis
sections.description: Probability bars for each pattern — NO direct disease diagnosis language
sections.ui: Horizontal progress bars with pattern name, probability %, confidence level
sections.disclaimer: 'These patterns are statistical indicators, not medical diagnoses.'
sections.patterns_displayed: All patterns from Model 2 output, sorted by probability descending
sections.chart: Optional Recharts RadarChart for multi-pattern visualization

sections.name: AI Health Reasoning
sections.description: Gemini-generated explainable reasoning — displayed as a professional medical interpretation
sections.gemini_attribution: NEVER shown — no 'Powered by Gemini', no AI branding
sections.example_content: The analysis indicates elevated fever risk consistent with a prolonged febrile illness. Key contributing factors include fever duration beyond 3 days, positive travel history, and documented sweating and muscle pain. SpO2 levels remain stable, reducing respiratory concern.

sections.name: Vital Signs Summary
sections.description: Cards for each sensor reading with normal range indicators

sections.name: Symptom Burden Index
sections.description: Calculated from severity scores — separate from ML output
sections.calculation: Sum of all severity_value scores (max = 24 for 8 symptoms × 3)

Severe (17–24).message: Heavy symptom burden — medical review needed

sections.ui: Horizontal health meter with animated fill + label

sections.name: Smart Recommendations
sections.tone: Human, clear, medically styled — not robotic

sections.name: Emergency Alerts
sections.description: Displayed prominently only when thresholds are exceeded

trigger_conditions.message: Dangerously low oxygen levels detected. Seek emergency care immediately.
Very high fever detected. Seek immediate medical attention.
Significantly elevated heart rate. Emergency evaluation recommended.
Prolonged fever requires medical evaluation. Schedule a doctor visit today.
Below normal oxygen saturation. Doctor consultation advised.

sections.ui: Top-of-results sticky alert banner (red for critical, amber for moderate) with pulsing border animation and 'Find Emergency Care' CTA

nearby_doctors_screen.description: Doctors and clinics are filtered based on your triage output fever type

Scrollable list below map — similar design to reference image (name, specialty, rating, location, distance, 'Get Directions' button)

card_fields: Get Directions button (links to Google Maps)

ui_layout.chips: Derived from fever_type_to_specialty_mapping for detected pattern

nearby_doctors_screen.note: NO appointment booking implemented. Only discovery and navigation.

trends_screen.subheader: Track your recovery and health progression over time

time_range_selector.ui: Pill chip selector at top of page

charts.title: Symptom Burden Score Over Time

charts.annotation: Recovery trend indicator (arrow + text if scores decreasing)

trend_indicators.improving: Green downward arrow icon + 'Recovery trend detected'
trend_indicators.stable: Blue horizontal arrow icon + 'Condition appears stable'
trend_indicators.worsening: Red upward arrow icon + 'Worsening trend — consider medical review'

scan_history_list.title: Scan History
scan_history_list.items: All scans sorted newest first — clickable to view full results

sections: Editable profile fields (age, gender, blood group)
Medical History quick view + edit link

sections.name: Patient List

Trend charts (same as patient trends screen)

routers/: scans.py — scan submission and retrieval endpoints
models/: risk_model.pkl — Random Forest Risk Level model
infection_model.pkl — Random Forest Infection Pattern model
services/: gemini_service.py — all Gemini API calls (never exposed externally)
supabase_service.py — Supabase client and CRUD operations
alerts_service.py — threshold checking and alert generation
schemas/: scan_schemas.py — Pydantic models for scan input/output
user_schemas.py — Pydantic models for user data
result_schemas.py — Pydantic models for ML + AI output
utils/: ppg_processor.py — optional server-side PPG signal processing
symptom_mapper.py — severity to binary value mapping

api_endpoints.auth_middleware: All protected routes require Supabase JWT in Authorization header

endpoints.description: Receive full scan data, run ML inference, call Gemini, store in Supabase, return result
Fetch all scans for authenticated patient
Proxy request to ESP32 — fetch sensor readings
Fetch daily_trends data for chart rendering
Get trend direction (improving/stable/worsening) based on last N scans
Save or update patient medical history
Fetch patient medical history for pre-fill

ml_inference_flow.step_2: Extract binary symptom features array for ML models
ml_inference_flow.step_4: Load infection_model.pkl → predict infection probabilities array
ml_inference_flow.step_6: Check alert thresholds (SpO2, temperature, HR, fever duration)
ml_inference_flow.step_7: Call gemini_service.generate_reasoning() with all outputs + medical history context
ml_inference_flow.step_9: Store scan in Supabase scans table
ml_inference_flow.step_10: Upsert daily_trends table for today's date

gemini_service.never_exposed_to: Frontend, client, browser, network response headers
gemini_service.system_prompt: You are a clinical AI assistant generating structured health triage reasoning. You must: (1) explain the predicted risk and infection patterns in professional medical language, (2) reference the patient's symptom profile and vitals as evidence, (3) generate 4-6 actionable health recommendations, (4) never directly diagnose a disease, (5) always recommend professional medical consultation for Moderate and above risk levels. Do not mention AI, Gemini, or any technology. Output must be structured JSON.

expected_output_json.recommendations: array of 4-6 recommendation objects { icon, title, description }
expected_output_json.recovery_guidance: string — what to monitor over the next 24-48 hours

gemini_service.fallback: If Gemini API fails, use pre-written rule-based templates per risk level

src/.App.jsx: Router setup with protected route wrapper
constants/: feverSpecialtyMap.js — fever type to doctor specialty map

frontend_.env.VITE_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY: Your Google Maps JavaScript API key

backend_.env.GEMINI_API_KEY: Your Gemini API key (NEVER expose to frontend)

api_keys: GEMINI_API_KEY: backend .env only — never in frontend bundle
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: backend only — use anon key on frontend
ESP32_ENDPOINT: can be in frontend for direct fetch, but prefer backend proxy for production
authentication: All API endpoints except health check require valid Supabase JWT
Patient can only access own data — enforced via RLS in Supabase
data_privacy: Medical history is only used as Gemini context — never returned raw in API responses to unauthorized users
PPG signal data stored as compressed jsonb — not exposed publicly
All Supabase tables have RLS enabled

ml_model_feature_vector.description: Exact feature array sent to both ML models

daily_trends_update_logic.trigger: Every time a scan is submitted successfully
daily_trends_update_logic.operation: UPSERT into daily_trends WHERE patient_id = X AND date = TODAY()
daily_trends_update_logic.aggregation: Average of all scans on the same day for numeric fields
daily_trends_update_logic.risk_level_aggregation: Maximum severity across all scans on the day

alert_system.evaluation_order: Check thresholds → generate alert objects → store in scans.alerts_triggered → display on results screen

ppg_technical_spec.signal_channel: Green channel mean (most sensitive to blood volume changes)

signal_processing.hrv_calculation: RMSSD of successive inter-peak intervals in milliseconds

quality_indicators.low_quality_warning: Show if SNR < threshold — 'Signal too weak, ensure finger covers lens'

storage.raw_signal: Downsample to 10 fps before storing (600 points for 60s)

google_maps_integration.api_used: Google Maps JavaScript API + Places API (Nearby Search)
google_maps_integration.filtering: Filter by specialty keywords derived from detected fever type
google_maps_integration.map_style: Clean minimal map style — light theme, suppress points of interest clutter
google_maps_integration.fallback: If geolocation denied, show a location search input

SymptomCard.chip_row: Horizontally scrollable pill chips below description

RiskLevelBanner.layout: Full-width card with left colored border (4px) + soft tinted background
RiskLevelBanner.text_layout: Risk level label (28px bold) + message below

BottomNav.active_item: Teal icon + small dot indicator below

StepProgress.style: Linear pill progress bar + step label + step count

animations_spec.page_load: Framer Motion AnimatePresence with fade + translateY(-12px → 0) per page
animations_spec.card_stagger: Each card has initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 20 }} animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }} transition={{ delay: index * 0.08 }}
animations_spec.chart_entry: All Recharts charts use animationBegin=200 animationDuration=1000
animations_spec.pulse_icon: CSS: @keyframes ping { 0% { transform: scale(1); opacity: 1 } 100% { transform: scale(1.6); opacity: 0 } }
animations_spec.ppg_waveform: Recharts LineChart updating every 100ms with last 200 data points — isAnimationActive=false for performance
animations_spec.progress_bar_fill: CSS transition: width 0.8s ease-in-out on mount
animations_spec.alert_banner: Framer Motion slide-down from top + red pulsing border

final_deliverables_checklist: ✅ Full React.js frontend (Vite) with Tailwind CSS
✅ Supabase Auth — Google OAuth patient login with persistent history
✅ Supabase PostgreSQL schema with all tables and RLS policies
✅ FastAPI backend with all endpoints
✅ Gemini API integration — internal reasoning layer, zero UI exposure
✅ ML model inference pipeline (Random Forest × 2)
✅ Medical History form with pre-population from Supabase
✅ Full symptom questionnaire with binary + severity mapping
✅ Hardware data screen — ESP32 fetch + manual entry tab switcher
✅ Camera PPG scan page with live waveform and HRV calculation
✅ Comprehensive results page with all 8 sections
✅ Nearby Doctors page filtered by fever type — Google Maps + Places API
✅ Daily + multi-week trend charts (5 chart types)
✅ Emergency alert system with threshold logic
✅ Smart recommendation cards (Gemini-powered, invisible)
✅ Doctor dashboard with patient management
✅ Mobile-first design matching reference UI style (white/navy/teal)
✅ Bottom navigation bar for mobile
✅ Framer Motion animations throughout
✅ Environment variable support for all API keys and endpoints
✅ Modular component architecture
✅ Professional production-style healthcare UI — NOT a student project

design_reference_notes.reference_image_analysis: The provided reference images show a healthcare booking app with: white cards on light blue-gray background, navy blue (#1B4FD8 approx) CTA buttons, pill-shaped time selector chips, horizontal date picker with circular selected date highlight, bottom navigation bar with 4 icons, doctor profile cards with real photos, star ratings, specialty chips (Neurology, Dentistry, Gynaecology), search bar with filter icon, notification bell — ALL of these design patterns must be replicated in NIDAN-AI's interface adapted for triage context
design_reference_notes.chip_style: Rounded pill chips: selected = navy blue fill + white text, unselected = light gray fill + dark gray text — exactly as in reference
design_reference_notes.card_style: White background, 16px border-radius, 8px padding, 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.06) shadow
design_reference_notes.button_style: Primary CTA: navy blue fill #1B4FD8, white text, 12-14px border-radius, bold font, subtle shadow
design_reference_notes.bottom_bar_style: Pure white, border-top, 4 items with icon + label, active item has colored icon and dot
design_reference_notes.typography_match: Clean sans-serif — DM Sans recommended to match the clean aesthetic in reference images
